original_image_tags: Black-and-white photograph of a typewritten envelope against a dark background. The envelope is addressed to "Mr Dominic Gerardi" and has a return address from "Director of Operations, Electronics Service Unit 4". A postmark is visible in the upper right. Handwritten annotations are present on the envelope and at the bottom of the photograph.
A 3-cent U.S. Postage stamp featuring the Statue of Liberty. The word "LIBERTY" is visible below the statue.

elements.description: One photograph of a dark-colored envelope. The envelope has typed return address text: 'Director of Operations Electronics Service Unit 4 Route 2 Roseau, Minnesota'. A circular postmark stamp is visible with text: 'ROSEAU MINN. JUL 21 7:30 AM 1958'. A U.S. postage stamp is affixed, showing 'U.S. POSTAGE 3c' and an image of the Statue of Liberty. The recipient address is typed: 'Mr Pomonic Gerardi Torrington, Connecticut'. A handwritten annotation '183 Harrington Ave' is above the recipient address. A faint handwritten notation 'B-2' is in the lower left corner. The envelope appears to be placed on a dark, textured background.

judge.reason: Description A claims the envelope is addressed to 'Mr Dominic Gerardi' while Description B reads 'Mr Pomonic Gerardi', contradicting the specific quoted text of the recipient's name.
